# API Configuration Profiles | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

API Configuration Profiles allow you to create and switch between different sets of AI settings. Each profile can have different configurations for each mode, letting you optimize your experience based on the task at hand.

info

Having multiple configuration profiles lets you quickly switch between different AI providers, models, and settings without reconfiguring everything each time you want to change your setup.

How It Works[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Configuration profiles can have their own:

*   API providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Glama, etc.)
*   API keys and authentication details
*   Model selections (o3-mini-high, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeek R1, etc.)
*   [Temperature settings](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/model-temperature)
     for controlling response randomness
*   Thinking budgets
*   Provider-specific settings

Note that available settings vary by provider and model. Each provider offers different configuration options, and even within the same provider, different models may support different parameter ranges or features.

Creating and Managing Profiles[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#creating-and-managing-profiles "Direct link to Creating and Managing Profiles")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Creating a Profile[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#creating-a-profile "Direct link to Creating a Profile")

1.  Open Settings by clicking the gear icon → Providers
2.  Click the "+" button next to the profile selector

![Profile selector with plus button](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-1.png)

3.  Enter a name for your new profile

![Creating a new profile dialog](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles.png)

4.  Configure the profile settings:
    
    *   Select your API provider
        
        ![Provider selection dropdown](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-2.png)
    *   Enter API key
        
        ![API key entry field](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-3.png)
    *   Choose a model
        
        ![Model selection interface](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-8.png)
    *   Adjust model parameters
        
        ![Model parameter adjustment controls](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-5.png)

### Switching Profiles[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#switching-profiles "Direct link to Switching Profiles")

Switch profiles in two ways:

1.  From Settings panel: Select a different profile from the dropdown

![Profile selection dropdown in Settings](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-7.png)

2.  During chat: Access the API Configuration dropdown in the chat interface

![API Configuration dropdown in chat interface](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-6.png)

### Pinning and Sorting Profiles[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#pinning-and-sorting-profiles "Direct link to Pinning and Sorting Profiles")

The API configuration dropdown now supports pinning your favorite profiles for quicker access:

1.  Hover over any profile in the dropdown to reveal the pin icon
2.  Click the pin icon to add the profile to your pinned list
3.  Pinned profiles appear at the top of the dropdown, sorted alphabetically
4.  Unpinned profiles appear below a separator, also sorted alphabetically
5.  You can unpin a profile by clicking the same icon again

![Pinning API configuration profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-4.png)

This feature makes it easier to navigate between commonly used profiles, especially when you have many configurations.

### Editing and Deleting Profiles[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#editing-and-deleting-profiles "Direct link to Editing and Deleting Profiles")

![Profile editing interface](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/api-configuration-profiles/api-configuration-profiles-10.png)

*   Select the profile in Settings to modify any settings
*   Click the pencil icon to rename a profile
*   Click the trash icon to delete a profile (you cannot delete the only remaining profile)

Linking Profiles to Modes[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#linking-profiles-to-modes "Direct link to Linking Profiles to Modes")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the Prompts tab, you can explicitly associate a specific Configuration Profile with each Mode. The system also automatically remembers which profile you last used with each mode, making your workflow more efficient.

Watch this demonstration of how to connect configuration profiles with specific modes for optimized workflows:

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Security Note[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#security-note "Direct link to Security Note")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

API keys are stored securely in VSCode's Secret Storage and are never exposed in plain text.

Related Features[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#related-features "Direct link to Related Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Works with [custom modes](https://kilo.ai/docs/agent-behavior/custom-modes)
     you create
*   Integrates with [local models](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/local-models)
     for offline work
*   Supports [temperature settings](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/model-temperature)
     per mode
*   Enhances cost management with [rate limits and usage tracking](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/rate-limits-costs)
    

*   [How It Works](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#how-it-works)
    
*   [Creating and Managing Profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#creating-and-managing-profiles)
    *   [Creating a Profile](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#creating-a-profile)
        
    *   [Switching Profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#switching-profiles)
        
    *   [Pinning and Sorting Profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#pinning-and-sorting-profiles)
        
    *   [Editing and Deleting Profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#editing-and-deleting-profiles)
        
*   [Linking Profiles to Modes](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#linking-profiles-to-modes)
    
*   [Security Note](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#security-note)
    
*   [Related Features](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles#related-features)
    

* * *

Is this documentation incorrect or incomplete? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/apps/kilocode-docs/issues/new?title=Documentation%20Issue:%20%2Fdocs%2Ffeatures%2Fapi-configuration-profiles)

# Managing Your Team | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Every person on the team is an _Owner_ or a _Member_.

Owners have full administrative oversight including billing, seat allocation, and model/provider selection.

Only Owners can conduct team management activities.

Members can use the Kilo Code extension and see data on the team's usage in the [usage dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/analytics)
.

Adding Team Members[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#adding-team-members "Direct link to Adding Team Members")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Navigate to Organization Tab** in your profile page and click on the team you want to manage
2.  **Click "Invite Member"** button
3.  **Enter the team member's email address**
4.  **Select initial role** (Member or Owner)
5.  Click **Send Invitation**

![invite-member](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3ca0391-8a61-457f-b38b-11f03b219b4f)

Removing Team Members[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#removing-team-members "Direct link to Removing Team Members")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When team members leave:

1.  **Navigate to Organization tab**
2.  **Find the departing member**
3.  **Click "Remove" button**
4.  **Confirm removal**
5.  **Seat becomes available** immediately

Changing Team Member Roles[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#changing-team-member-roles "Direct link to Changing Team Member Roles")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Promote or demote team members as needed:

1.  **Locate team member** in Organization tab
2.  **Click role dropdown** next to their name
3.  **Select new role** (Member, Owner)
4.  **Confirm change**
5.  **Member receives email notification**

### Viewing Team Status[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#viewing-team-status "Direct link to Viewing Team Status")

The Organization tab shows:

*   **Active members** with last activity
*   **Pending invitations** awaiting acceptance
*   **Role distribution** across the team

Next Steps[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Understand billing and credits](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/billing)
    
*   [Explore usage analytics](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/analytics)
    
*   [Learn about team roles and permissions](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management)
    

Effective team management ensures your organization maximizes the benefits of AI-assisted development while maintaining cost control and security.

*   [Adding Team Members](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#adding-team-members)
    
*   [Removing Team Members](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#removing-team-members)
    
*   [Changing Team Member Roles](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#changing-team-member-roles)
    *   [Viewing Team Status](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#viewing-team-status)
        
*   [Next Steps](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/team-management#next-steps)
    

* * *

Is this documentation incorrect or incomplete? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/apps/kilocode-docs/issues/new?title=Documentation%20Issue:%20%2Fdocs%2Fplans%2Fteam-management)

# Using Kilo Code for Free and on a Budget | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

**Why this matters:** AI model costs can add up quickly during development. This guide shows you how to use Kilo Code effectively while minimizing or eliminating costs through free models, budget-friendly alternatives, and smart usage strategies.

Completely Free Options[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#completely-free-options "Direct link to Completely Free Options")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Grok Code Fast 1[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#grok-code-fast-1 "Direct link to Grok Code Fast 1")

This frontier AI model is 100% free in Kilo Code for a limited time. [See the blog post for more details](https://blog.kilo.ai/p/grok-code-fast-get-this-frontier-ai-model-free)
.

### OpenRouter Free Tier Models[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#openrouter-free-tier-models "Direct link to OpenRouter Free Tier Models")

OpenRouter offers several models with generous free tiers. **Note:** You'll need to create a free OpenRouter account to access these models.

**Setup:**

1.  Create a free [OpenRouter account](https://openrouter.ai/)
    
2.  Get your API key from the dashboard
3.  Configure Kilo Code with the OpenRouter provider

**Available free models:**

*   **Qwen3 Coder (free)** - Optimized for agentic coding tasks such as function calling, tool use, and long-context reasoning over repositories.
*   **Z.AI: GLM 4.5 Air (free)** - Lightweight variant of the GLM-4.5 family, purpose-built for agent-centric applications.
*   **DeepSeek: R1 0528 (free)** - Performance on par with OpenAI o1, but open-sourced and with fully open reasoning tokens.
*   **MoonshotAI: Kimi K2 (free)** - Optimized for agentic capabilities, including advanced tool use, reasoning, and code synthesis.

Cost-Effective Premium Models[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#cost-effective-premium-models "Direct link to Cost-Effective Premium Models")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you need more capability than free models provide, these options deliver excellent value:

### Ultra-Budget Champions (Under $0.50 per million tokens)[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#ultra-budget-champions-under-050-per-million-tokens "Direct link to Ultra-Budget Champions (Under $0.50 per million tokens)")

**Mistral Devstral Small**

*   **Cost:** ~$0.20 per million input tokens
*   **Best for:** Code generation, debugging, refactoring
*   **Performance:** 85% of premium model capability at 10% of the cost

**Llama 4 Maverick**

*   **Cost:** ~$0.30 per million input tokens
*   **Best for:** Complex reasoning, architecture planning
*   **Performance:** Excellent for most development tasks

**DeepSeek v3**

*   **Cost:** ~$0.27 per million input tokens
*   **Best for:** Code analysis, large codebase understanding
*   **Performance:** Strong technical reasoning

### Mid-Range Value Models ($0.50-$2.00 per million tokens)[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#mid-range-value-models-050-200-per-million-tokens "Direct link to Mid-Range Value Models ($0.50-$2.00 per million tokens)")

**Qwen3 235B**

*   **Cost:** ~$1.20 per million input tokens
*   **Best for:** Complex projects requiring high accuracy
*   **Performance:** Near-premium quality at 40% of the cost

Smart Usage Strategies[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#smart-usage-strategies "Direct link to Smart Usage Strategies")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The 50% Rule[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#the-50-rule "Direct link to The 50% Rule")

**Principle:** Use budget models for 50% of your tasks, premium models for the other 50%.

**Budget model tasks:**

*   Code reviews and analysis
*   Documentation writing
*   Simple bug fixes
*   Boilerplate generation
*   Refactoring existing code

**Premium model tasks:**

*   Complex architecture decisions
*   Debugging difficult issues
*   Performance optimization
*   New feature design
*   Critical production code

### Context Management for Cost Savings[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#context-management-for-cost-savings "Direct link to Context Management for Cost Savings")

**Minimize context size:**

    // Instead of mentioning entire files@src/components/UserProfile.tsx// Mention specific functions or sections@src/components/UserProfile.tsx:45-67

**Use Memory Bank effectively:**

*   Store project context once in [Memory Bank](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/memory-bank)
    
*   Reduces need to re-explain project details
*   Saves 200-500 tokens per conversation

**Strategic file mentions:**

*   Only include files directly relevant to the task
*   Use [`@folder/`](https://kilo.ai/docs/basic-usage/context-mentions)
     for broad context, specific files for targeted work

### Model Switching Strategies[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#model-switching-strategies "Direct link to Model Switching Strategies")

**Start cheap, escalate when needed:**

1.  **Begin with free models** (Qwen3 Coder, GLM-4.5-Air)
2.  **Switch to budget models** if free models struggle
3.  **Escalate to premium models** only for complex tasks

**Use API Configuration Profiles:**

*   Set up [multiple profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles)
     for different cost tiers
*   Quick switching between free, budget, and premium models
*   Match model capability to task complexity

### Mode-Based Cost Optimization[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#mode-based-cost-optimization "Direct link to Mode-Based Cost Optimization")

**Use appropriate modes to limit expensive operations:**

*   **[Ask Mode](https://kilo.ai/docs/basic-usage/using-modes#ask-mode)
    :** Information gathering without code changes
*   **[Architect Mode](https://kilo.ai/docs/basic-usage/using-modes#architect-mode)
    :** Planning without expensive file operations
*   **[Debug Mode](https://kilo.ai/docs/basic-usage/using-modes#debug-mode)
    :** Focused troubleshooting

**Custom modes for budget control:**

*   Create modes that restrict expensive tools
*   Limit file access to specific directories
*   Control which operations are auto-approved

Real-World Performance Comparisons[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#real-world-performance-comparisons "Direct link to Real-World Performance Comparisons")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Code Generation Tasks[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#code-generation-tasks "Direct link to Code Generation Tasks")

**Simple function creation:**

*   **Mistral Devstral Small:** 95% success rate
*   **GPT-4:** 98% success rate
*   **Cost difference:** Free vs $0.20 vs $30 per million tokens

**Complex refactoring:**

*   **Budget models:** 70-80% success rate
*   **Premium models:** 90-95% success rate
*   **Recommendation:** Start with budget, escalate if needed

### Debugging Performance[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#debugging-performance "Direct link to Debugging Performance")

**Simple bugs:**

*   **Free models:** Usually sufficient
*   **Budget models:** Excellent performance
*   **Premium models:** Overkill for most cases

**Complex system issues:**

*   **Free models:** 40-60% success rate
*   **Budget models:** 60-80% success rate
*   **Premium models:** 85-95% success rate

Hybrid Approach Recommendations[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#hybrid-approach-recommendations "Direct link to Hybrid Approach Recommendations")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Daily Development Workflow[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#daily-development-workflow "Direct link to Daily Development Workflow")

**Morning planning session:**

*   Use **Architect mode** with **DeepSeek R1**
*   Plan features and architecture
*   Create task breakdowns

**Implementation phase:**

*   Use **Code mode** with **budget models**
*   Generate and modify code
*   Handle routine development tasks

**Complex problem solving:**

*   Switch to **premium models** when stuck
*   Use for critical debugging
*   Architecture decisions affecting multiple systems

### Project Phase Strategy[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#project-phase-strategy "Direct link to Project Phase Strategy")

**Early development:**

*   Free and budget models for prototyping
*   Rapid iteration without cost concerns
*   Establish patterns and structure

**Production preparation:**

*   Premium models for critical code review
*   Performance optimization
*   Security considerations

Cost Monitoring and Control[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#cost-monitoring-and-control "Direct link to Cost Monitoring and Control")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Track Your Usage[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#track-your-usage "Direct link to Track Your Usage")

**Monitor credit consumption:**

*   Check cost estimates in chat history
*   Review monthly usage patterns
*   Identify high-cost operations

**Set spending limits:**

*   Use provider billing alerts
*   Configure [rate limits](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/rate-limits-costs)
     to control usage
*   Set daily/monthly budgets

### Cost-Saving Tips[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#cost-saving-tips "Direct link to Cost-Saving Tips")

**Reduce system prompt size:**

*   [Disable MCP](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/mcp/using-mcp-in-kilo-code)
     if not using external tools
*   Use focused custom modes
*   Minimize unnecessary context

**Optimize conversation length:**

*   Use [Checkpoints](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/checkpoints)
     to reset context
*   Start fresh conversations for unrelated tasks
*   Archive completed work

**Batch similar tasks:**

*   Group related code changes
*   Handle multiple files in single requests
*   Reduce conversation overhead

Getting Started with Budget Models[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#getting-started-with-budget-models "Direct link to Getting Started with Budget Models")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Quick Setup Guide[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#quick-setup-guide "Direct link to Quick Setup Guide")

1.  **Create OpenRouter account** for free models
2.  **Configure multiple providers** in Kilo Code
3.  **Set up API Configuration Profiles** for easy switching
4.  **Escalate to budget models** when needed
5.  **Reserve premium models** for complex work

### Recommended Provider Mix[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#recommended-provider-mix "Direct link to Recommended Provider Mix")

**Free tier foundation:**

*   [OpenRouter](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/openrouter)
     - Free models
*   [Groq](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/groq)
     - Fast inference for supported models
*   [Z.ai](https://z.ai/model-api)
     - Provides a free model GLM-4.5-Flash

**Budget tier options:**

*   [DeepSeek](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/deepseek)
     - Excellent value models
*   [Mistral](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/mistral)
     - Specialized coding models

**Premium tier backup:**

*   [Anthropic](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/anthropic)
     - Claude for complex reasoning
*   [OpenAI](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/openai)
     - GPT-4 for critical tasks

Measuring Success[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#measuring-success "Direct link to Measuring Success")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Track these metrics:**

*   Monthly AI costs vs. development productivity
*   Task completion rates by model tier
*   Time saved vs. money spent
*   Code quality improvements

**Success indicators:**

*   70%+ of tasks completed with free/budget models
*   Monthly costs under your target budget
*   Maintained or improved code quality
*   Faster development cycles

By combining free models, strategic budget model usage, and smart optimization techniques, you can harness the full power of AI-assisted development while keeping costs minimal. Start with free options and gradually incorporate budget models as your needs and comfort with costs grow.

*   [Completely Free Options](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#completely-free-options)
    *   [Grok Code Fast 1](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#grok-code-fast-1)
        
    *   [OpenRouter Free Tier Models](https://kilo.ai/docs/advanced-usage/free-and-budget-models#openrouter-free-tier-models)

# Single Sign-On (SSO) (Enterprise Only) | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Kilo Enterprise lets your organization securely manage access using **Single Sign-On (SSO)**. With SSO enabled, team members can sign in to Kilo using your company’s existing identity provider, such as Okta, Github, Google Workspace, etc.

Why Enable SSO?[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#why-enable-sso "Direct link to Why Enable SSO?")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Centralized access control:** Manage permissions through your existing identity provider.
*   **Faster onboarding:** New team members automatically gain access when added to your company directory.
*   **Improved security:** Enforce company-wide password, MFA, and session policies.
*   **Easy offboarding:** Smoothly remove access when users leave your organization.

Prerequisites[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You’ll need:

*   Admin or Owner permissions for your Kilo organization.
*   Access to your **Identity Provider (IdP)** (e.g. Okta, Google Workspace, Azure AD).

Step-by-Step Setup[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#step-by-step-setup "Direct link to Step-by-Step Setup")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1\. Open [Organization](https://app.kilo.ai/organizations)
 Dashboard[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#1-open-organization-dashboard "Direct link to 1-open-organization-dashboard")

Find the Single Sign-On (SSO) Configuration panel, and click "Set up SSO":

![Set-up-SSO screen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6ca5f83-4533-4d41-bcb1-0038b645c030)

### 2\. Submit the SSO Request Form[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#2-submit-the-sso-request-form "Direct link to 2. Submit the SSO Request Form")

Fill in your contact information and someone from our team will reach out soon to help you configure SSO.

✅ Next Steps[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#-next-steps "Direct link to ✅ Next Steps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After enabling SSO:

*   Invite new users with their company email domain.
*   Manage team access and roles from the **[Organization](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/dashboard)
    ** tab.
*   View user activity across the team in the **[Audit Logs](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs)
    ** tab

*   [Why Enable SSO?](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#why-enable-sso)
    
*   [Prerequisites](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#prerequisites)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Setup](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#step-by-step-setup)
    *   [1\. Open Organization Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#1-open-organization-dashboard)
        
    *   [2\. Submit the SSO Request Form](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#2-submit-the-sso-request-form)
        
*   [✅ Next Steps](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO#-next-steps)
    

* * *

# Audit Logs (Enterprise Only) | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Audit Logs record key actions that occur in the management of your Kilo seats, including user logins, adding or removing models, providers, and modes, and role changes.

Owners and Admins can search and filter logs to review access patterns and ensure compliance.

Viewing Audit Logs[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#viewing-audit-logs "Direct link to Viewing Audit Logs")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Only **Owners** can view and filter through logs.

Go to **Enterprise Dashboard → Audit Logs** to view a searchable history of all organization events. Use filters to narrow down results by action, user, or date range.

![Audit-log-dashboard](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/41fcf43f-4a47-4f47-a3d9-02d20a6427a6)

Filters[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#filters "Direct link to Filters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Filter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Actions** | Choose one or more events to view. Options include:  <br>\- `user login` / `logout`  <br>\- `user invite`, `accept invite`, `revoke invite`  <br>\- `settings change`  <br>\- `purchase credits`  <br>\- `member remove`, `member change role`  <br>\- `sso set domain`, `sso remove domain` |
| **Actor Email** | Filter by the user who performed the action. |
| **Start / End Date** | Specify a date and time range to view logs within that period. |

Multiple filters can be used together for precise auditing.

Log Details[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#log-details "Direct link to Log Details")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each event includes:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Time** | When the action occurred (shown in your local timezone). |
| **Action** | The event type (e.g. `user.login`, `settings.change`). |
| **Actor** | The user who performed the action. |
| **Details** | Context or additional data related to the event (e.g. models added or removed). |

Logged Events[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#logged-events "Direct link to Logged Events")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here is the list of all events included in the Kilo Code audit logs:

*   Organization: Create, Settings Change, Purchase Credits
*   Organization Member: Remove, Change Role
*   User: Login, Logout, Accept Invite, Send Invite, Revoke Invite
*   [Custom Modes](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/custom-modes)
    : Create, Update, Delete
*   [SSO](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/SSO)
     (Enterprise Only): Auto Provision, Set Domain, Remove Domain

*   [Viewing Audit Logs](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#viewing-audit-logs)
    
*   [Filters](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#filters)
    
*   [Log Details](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#log-details)
    
*   [Logged Events](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/audit-logs#logged-events)
    

* * *

Is this documentation incorrect or incomplete? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/apps/kilocode-docs/issues/new?title=Documentation%20Issue:%20%2Fdocs%2Fplans%2Fenterprise%2Faudit-logs)

# Model Access (Enterprise Only) | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

**Model Access** lets organization admins control which AI models and providers are available to team members. Admins can **enable or disable** specific models, filter by attributes, and enforce organizational data policies.

Managing Model Access[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#managing-model-access "Direct link to Managing Model Access")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Navigate to the **Model Access** tab of the Enterprise Dashboard.
2.  Toggle the checkbox beside any model or provider to enable or disable access.
3.  Click "Save Changes" to apply

![Model-Access-Select](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af71353d-facc-4d4b-a0cd-c7f2cea73e97)

Filtering Models[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#filtering-models "Direct link to Filtering Models")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can filter available models by:

| Filter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Data Policy** | Choose models that meet specific data retention or compliance requirements. |
| **Provider Location** | Restrict models hosted in certain geographic regions. |
| **Series** | Filter by model family (e.g. GPT-4, Claude 3, Gemini 1.5). |
| **Provider** | Limit access to specific providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google. |
| **Input / Output Modalities** | Filter by capabilities (text, code, image, audio, etc.). |
| **Pricing** | Compare cost per token or usage tier. |

Select multiple filters for increased granularity.

* * *

Example Use Cases[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#example-use-cases "Direct link to Example Use Cases")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Security-first teams**: Disable models that store prompts or operate outside your data region.
*   **Cost control**: Limit access to higher-priced models.
*   **Specialization**: Enable models that are optimized for specific tasks.

* * *

Notes[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#notes "Direct link to Notes")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Only **Admins** and **Owners** can modify model access.
*   Updates propagate to all team members within seconds.
*   Individual users cannot override organization-level restrictions.

*   [Managing Model Access](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#managing-model-access)
    
*   [Filtering Models](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#filtering-models)
    
*   [Example Use Cases](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#example-use-cases)
    
*   [Notes](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/enterprise/model-access#notes)
    

* * *

# Additional Features | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Kilo Code's extras streamline routine tasks and improve accessibility.

Suggested Responses[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#suggested-responses "Direct link to Suggested Responses")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Kilo Code offers suggested responses so you spend less time typing.

*   After you ask a question, buttons appear below the chat box.
*   Click a button to reuse it as your next prompt.

Text to Speech[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#text-to-speech "Direct link to Text to Speech")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Text-to-Speech feature lets Kilo Code read responses aloud.

1.  Enable TTS in settings.
2.  Click the speaker icon next to any response to start listening.

Global Language Support[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#global-language-support "Direct link to Global Language Support")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Kilo Code supports 14 languages:

*   Simplified Chinese
*   Traditional Chinese
*   Spanish
*   Hindi
*   French
*   Portuguese
*   German
*   Japanese
*   Korean
*   Italian
*   Turkish
*   Vietnamese
*   Polish
*   Catalan

Change languages under **Advanced Settings > Language**.

*   [Suggested Responses](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#suggested-responses)
    
*   [Text to Speech](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/more-features#text-to-speech)

# Tips & Tricks | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/tips-and-tricks#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

A collection of quick tips to help you get the most out of Kilo Code.

*   Right-click on the Kilo Code icon in the Activity Bar and select **Move To → Secondary Side Bar** to move Kilo Code to the [Secondary Sidebar](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/ux-guidelines/sidebars#secondary-sidebar)
    , so you can see the Explorer, Search, Source Control, etc. alongside Kilo Code.
    
    ![Move to Secondary Side Bar](https://kilo.ai/docs/assets/images/move-to-secondary-f8fa21f6972aaa5bcc6eeceef76f4c43.png)
    
*   Once you have Kilo Code in a separate sidebar from the file explorer, you can drag files from the explorer into the chat window (and even multiple at once). Just make sure to hold down the shift key after you start dragging the files.
    
*   **Autocomplete Tips:**
    
    *   Use chat for multi-file changes, refactoring, or when you need to explain intent. Use autocomplete for quick, localized edits where the context is already clear from surrounding code.
    *   Steer autocomplete by writing a comment describing what you want before triggering it, or by typing a function signature—autocomplete will fill in the implementation.
    *   Treat autocomplete suggestions as drafts: accept them quickly, then refine. It's often faster to fix a 90% correct suggestion than to craft the perfect prompt.
*   If you're not using [MCP](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/mcp/overview)
    , turn it off in the Prompts tab to significantly cut down the size of the system prompt.
    
*   To keep your [custom modes](https://kilo.ai/docs/agent-behavior/custom-modes)
     on track, limit the types of files that they're allowed to edit.
    
*   If you hit the dreaded `input length and max tokens exceed context limit` error, you can recover by deleting a message, rolling back to a previous checkpoint, or switching over to a model with a long context window like Gemini for a message.
    
*   In general, be thoughtful about your `Max Tokens` setting for thinking models. Every token you allocate to that takes away from space available to store conversation history. Consider only using high `Max Tokens` / `Max Thinking Tokens` settings with modes like Architect and Debug, and keeping Code mode at 16k max tokens or less.
    
*   If there's a real world job posting for something you want a custom mode to do, try asking Code mode to `Create a custom mode based on the job posting at @[url]`
    
*   If you want to really accelerate, check out multiple copies of your repository and run Kilo Code on all of them in parallel (using git to resolve any conflicts, same as with human devs).
    
*   When using Debug mode, ask Kilo to "start a new task in Debug mode with all of the necessary context needed to figure out X" so that the debugging process uses its own context window and doesn't pollute the main task
    
*   Add your own tips by clicking "Edit this page" below!
    
*   To manage large files and reduce context/resource usage, adjust the `File read auto-truncate threshold` setting. This setting controls the number of lines read from a file in one batch. Lower values can improve performance when working with very large files, but may require more read operations. You can find this setting in the Kilo Code settings under 'Advanced Settings'.
    

| Browser Tab | Auto-Approve Tab | Display Tab |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [![KiloCode Demo 1](https://img.youtube.com/vi/VMPKXt8k050/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://youtube.com/shorts/VMPKXt8k050?feature=shared) | [![KiloCode Demo 2](https://img.youtube.com/vi/NBccFnYDQ-k/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://youtube.com/shorts/NBccFnYDQ-k?feature=shared) | [![KiloCode Demo 3](https://img.youtube.com/vi/qYrT2pbfS7E/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://youtube.com/shorts/qYrT2pbfS7E?feature=shared) |

* * *

Is this documentation incorrect or incomplete? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/apps/kilocode-docs/issues/new?title=Documentation%20Issue:%20%2Fdocs%2Ftips-and-tricks)

# Auto-Launch Configuration | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Auto-Launch Configuration allows you to automatically start a Kilo Code task when opening a workspace, with support for specific profiles and modes. This was originally developed as an internal test feature, but we decided to expose it to users in case anyone finds it useful!

info

Auto-Launch Configuration is particularly useful for testing the same prompt against multiple models or project directories.

How It Works[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you open a workspace in VS Code, Kilo Code automatically checks for a launch configuration JSON file. If found, it:

*   Switches to the specified provider profile (if provided)
*   Changes to the specified mode (if provided)
*   Launches a task with your predefined prompt

This happens seamlessly in the background, requiring no manual intervention.

Creating a Launch Configuration[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#creating-a-launch-configuration "Direct link to Creating a Launch Configuration")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Setup[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#basic-setup "Direct link to Basic Setup")

1.  Create a `.kilocode` directory in your workspace root (if it doesn't exist)
2.  Create a `launchConfig.json` file inside the `.kilocode` directory
3.  Configure your launch settings using the JSON format below

### Configuration Format[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#configuration-format "Direct link to Configuration Format")

    {	"prompt": "Your task description here",	"profile": "Profile Name (optional)",	"mode": "mode-name (optional)"}

#### Required Fields[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#required-fields "Direct link to Required Fields")

*   **`prompt`** (string): The task message that will be sent to the AI when the workspace opens

#### Optional Fields[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#optional-fields "Direct link to Optional Fields")

*   **`profile`** (string): Name of an existing [API Configuration Profile](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles)
     to use for this task. Must exactly match a profile name from your settings.
    
*   **`mode`** (string): The Kilo Code mode to use for this task. Available modes:
    
    *   `"code"` - General-purpose coding tasks
    *   `"architect"` - Planning and technical design
    *   `"ask"` - Questions and explanations
    *   `"debug"` - Problem diagnosis and troubleshooting
    *   `"test"` - Testing-focused workflows
    *   Custom mode slugs (if you have [custom modes](https://kilo.ai/docs/agent-behavior/custom-modes)
        )

Example Configurations[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#example-configurations "Direct link to Example Configurations")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Task Launch[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#basic-task-launch "Direct link to Basic Task Launch")

    {	"prompt": "Review this codebase and suggest improvements for performance and maintainability"}

### Profile-Specific Task[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#profile-specific-task "Direct link to Profile-Specific Task")

    {	"prompt": "Create comprehensive unit tests for all components in the src/ directory",	"profile": "GPT-4 Turbo"}

### Architecture Planning with Claude[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#architecture-planning-with-claude "Direct link to Architecture Planning with Claude")

    {	"prompt": "Design a scalable microservices architecture for this e-commerce platform with focus on security and performance",	"profile": "🎻 Sonnet 4",	"mode": "architect"}

### Model Comparison Setup[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#model-comparison-setup "Direct link to Model Comparison Setup")

    {	"prompt": "Optimize this algorithm for better time complexity and explain your approach",	"profile": "🧠 Qwen",	"mode": "code"}

Use Cases[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Development Workflows[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#development-workflows "Direct link to Development Workflows")

*   **Project Templates**: Include launch configurations in project templates to immediately start with appropriate AI assistance
*   **Code Reviews**: Automatically trigger code review tasks when opening pull request branches
*   **Documentation**: Launch documentation generation tasks for new projects

### Testing and Comparison[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#testing-and-comparison "Direct link to Testing and Comparison")

*   **Model Testing**: Create different configurations to test how various AI models handle the same prompt
*   **A/B Testing**: Compare approaches by switching between different profiles and modes
*   **Benchmarking**: Systematically test AI performance across different scenarios

### Team Collaboration[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#team-collaboration "Direct link to Team Collaboration")

*   **Consistent Setup**: Ensure all team members use the same AI configuration for specific projects
*   **Onboarding**: Help new team members start with optimal AI settings automatically
*   **Standards**: Enforce coding standards by launching with specific profiles and modes

File Location[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#file-location "Direct link to File Location")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The configuration file must be located at:

    your-workspace/└── .kilocode/    └── launchConfig.json

This file should be at the root of your workspace (the same level as your main project files).

Behavior and Timing[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#behavior-and-timing "Direct link to Behavior and Timing")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Auto-launch triggers approximately 500ms after Kilo Code extension activation
*   The sidebar automatically receives focus before the task launches
*   Profile switching happens before mode switching (if both are specified)
*   The task launches after all configuration changes are applied
*   If profile or mode switching fails, the task continues with current settings

Troubleshooting[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Configuration Not Loading[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#configuration-not-loading "Direct link to Configuration Not Loading")

1.  Verify file location: `.kilocode/launchConfig.json` in workspace root
2.  Check JSON syntax with a JSON validator
3.  Ensure `prompt` field is present and not empty
4.  Check VS Code Developer Console for error messages

### Profile Not Switching[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#profile-not-switching "Direct link to Profile Not Switching")

1.  Verify the profile name exactly matches one from your settings
2.  Profile names are case-sensitive and must match exactly (including emojis)
3.  Check that the profile exists in your [API Configuration Profiles](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/api-configuration-profiles)
    

### Mode Not Switching[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#mode-not-switching "Direct link to Mode Not Switching")

1.  Verify the mode name is valid (code, architect, ask, debug, test)
2.  For custom modes, use the exact mode slug from your configuration
3.  Mode names are case-sensitive and should be lowercase

*   [How It Works](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#how-it-works)
    
*   [Creating a Launch Configuration](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#creating-a-launch-configuration)
    *   [Basic Setup](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#basic-setup)
        
    *   [Configuration Format](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#configuration-format)
        
*   [Example Configurations](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#example-configurations)
    *   [Basic Task Launch](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#basic-task-launch)
        
    *   [Profile-Specific Task](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#profile-specific-task)
        
    *   [Architecture Planning with Claude](https://kilo.ai/docs/features/auto-launch-configuration#architecture-planning-with-claude)

# Connecting an AI Provider | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Kilo Code requires an API key from an AI model provider to function.

We recommend these options for accessing the powerful **Claude 4 Sonnet** model:

*   **Kilo Gateway (Recommended):** Provides access to all of the models available through OpenRouter with competitive pricing and free credits to get started. [View pricing](https://kilo.ai/pricing)
    
*   **OpenRouter:** Provides access to multiple AI models through a single API key. [View pricing](https://openrouter.ai/models?order=pricing-low-to-high)
    .
*   **Anthropic:** Direct access to Claude models. Requires API access approval and may have [rate limits depending on your tier](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/rate-limits#requirements-to-advance-tier)
    . See [Anthropic's pricing page](https://www.anthropic.com/pricing#anthropic-api)
     for details.

Using the Kilo Code Provider[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#using-the-kilo-code-provider "Direct link to Using the Kilo Code Provider")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default when you install Kilo Code the extension, you'll be prompted to sign in or create an account in the [Kilo Code Provider](https://kilo.ai/docs/providers/kilocode)
.

That will walk you through the account setup and _automatically_ configure Kilo Code properly to get you started. If you'd rather use another provider, you'll need to manually get your API key as described below.

Using another API Provider[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#using-another-api-provider "Direct link to Using another API Provider")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Coming soon to Kilo Code Teams and Enterprise!_

### Getting Your API Key[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#getting-your-api-key "Direct link to Getting Your API Key")

#### Option 1: LLM Routers[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#option-1-llm-routers "Direct link to Option 1: LLM Routers")

LLM routers let you access multiple AI models with one API key, simplifying cost management and switching between models. They often offer [competitive pricing](https://openrouter.ai/models?order=pricing-low-to-high)
 compared to direct providers.

##### OpenRouter[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#openrouter "Direct link to OpenRouter")

1.  Go to [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai/)
    
2.  Sign in with your Google or GitHub account
3.  Navigate to the [API keys page](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
     and create a new key
4.  Copy your API key - you'll need this for Kilo Code setup

![OpenRouter API keys page](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/connecting-api-provider/connecting-api-provider-4.png)

_OpenRouter dashboard with "Create key" button. Name your key and copy it after creation._

##### Requesty[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#requesty "Direct link to Requesty")

1.  Go to [requesty.ai](https://requesty.ai/)
    
2.  Sign in with your Google account or email
3.  Navigate to the [API management page](https://app.requesty.ai/manage-api)
     and create a new key
4.  **Important:** Copy your API key immediately as it won't be displayed again

![Requesty API management page](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/connecting-api-provider/connecting-api-provider-7.png)

_Requesty API management page with "Create API Key" button. Copy your key immediately - it's shown only once._

#### Option 2: Direct Providers[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#option-2-direct-providers "Direct link to Option 2: Direct Providers")

For direct access to specific models from their original providers, with full access to their features and capabilities:

##### Anthropic[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#anthropic "Direct link to Anthropic")

1.  Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com/)
    
2.  Sign up for an account or log in
3.  Navigate to the [API keys section](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)
     and create a new key
4.  **Important:** Copy your API key immediately as it won't be displayed again

![Anthropic console API Keys section](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/connecting-api-provider/connecting-api-provider-5.png)

_Anthropic console API Keys section with "Create key" button. Name your key, set expiration, and copy it immediately._

##### OpenAI[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#openai "Direct link to OpenAI")

1.  Go to [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com/)
    
2.  Sign up for an account or log in
3.  Navigate to the [API keys section](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
     and create a new key
4.  **Important:** Copy your API key immediately as it won't be displayed again

![OpenAI API keys page](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/connecting-api-provider/connecting-api-provider-6.png)

_OpenAI platform with "Create new secret key" button. Name your key and copy it immediately after creation._

### Configuring the Provider in Kilo Code[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#configuring-the-provider-in-kilo-code "Direct link to Configuring the Provider in Kilo Code")

Once you have your API key:

1.  Open the Kilo Code sidebar by clicking the Kilo Code icon (![](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/kilo-v1.svg)) in the VS Code Side Bar
2.  In the welcome screen, select your API provider from the dropdown
3.  Paste your API key into the appropriate field
4.  Select your model:
    *   For **OpenRouter**: select `anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet` ([model details](https://openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet)
        )
    *   For **Anthropic**: select `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` ([model details](https://www.anthropic.com/pricing#anthropic-api)
        )
5.  Click "Let's go!" to save your settings and start using Kilo Code

*   [Using the Kilo Code Provider](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#using-the-kilo-code-provider)
    
*   [Using another API Provider](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#using-another-api-provider)
    *   [Getting Your API Key](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#getting-your-api-key)
        
    *   [Configuring the Provider in Kilo Code](https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started/connecting-api-provider#configuring-the-provider-in-kilo-code)
        

* * *

Is this documentation incorrect or incomplete? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/tree/main/apps/kilocode-docs/issues/new?title=Documentation%20Issue:%20%2Fdocs%2Fgetting-started%2Fconnecting-api-provider)

# AI Adoption Dashboard | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The AI Adoption Dashboard helps engineering leaders understand how deeply and consistently their teams are using AI across development workflows. It provides a single **AI Adoption Score** (0–100) that quantifies organizational AI maturity, plus detailed breakdowns by dimension.

![Monthly view of the AI Adoption Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/ai-adoption/adoption-dashboard.png)

Who Is It For?[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#who-is-it-for "Direct link to Who Is It For?")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This dashboard is designed for **team leads, engineering managers, and executives** who want to:

*   Track AI integration progress across their organization
*   Compare teams using a single benchmark number
*   Identify low-, medium-, and high-adoption teams
*   Quote a simple metric to stakeholders ("We're at 63; we want to be at 80")

Accessing the Dashboard[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#accessing-the-dashboard "Direct link to Accessing the Dashboard")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Navigate to [app.kilo.ai](https://app.kilo.ai/)
     and sign in
2.  Select your organization
3.  Click the **Usage** tab in the dashboard navigation
4.  The AI Adoption Score card appears at the top of the usage view

Dashboard Overview[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#dashboard-overview "Direct link to Dashboard Overview")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Main Score Display[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#main-score-display "Direct link to Main Score Display")

The dashboard prominently displays your current AI Adoption Score as a percentage (e.g., "Current: 45%"). This score represents how deeply and consistently your organization uses AI across real development workflows.

### Timeline Visualization[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#timeline-visualization "Direct link to Timeline Visualization")

![Monthly view of the AI Adoption Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/ai-adoption/stacked-chart.png)

A stacked bar chart shows your daily adoption scores over time. The chart uses three colors representing the score's dimensions:

*   **Blue** — Frequency (how often developers use AI)
*   **Green** — Depth (how integrated AI is into development)
*   **Orange** — Coverage (how broadly AI is adopted across the team)

### Time Period Filters[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#time-period-filters "Direct link to Time Period Filters")

Filter the data by selecting:

*   **Past Week** — Last 7 days
*   **Past Month** — Last 30 days
*   **Past Year** — Last 365 days
*   **All** — Complete history

### Personal vs. Organization View[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#personal-vs-organization-view "Direct link to Personal vs. Organization View")

Use the **"Only my usage"** toggle to switch between:

*   **Enabled** — Your individual adoption metrics
*   **Disabled** — Organization-wide adoption metrics

### Trend Indicators[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#trend-indicators "Direct link to Trend Indicators")

Four metric cards at the bottom of the dashboard show week-over-week changes:

*   **Total** — Overall score trend
*   **Frequency** — Changes in usage frequency
*   **Depth** — Changes in integration depth
*   **Coverage** — Changes in team-wide adoption

Each card displays the percentage change (e.g., "+2.3%" or "-1.5%") with a directional indicator.

The Three Dimensions[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#the-three-dimensions "Direct link to The Three Dimensions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The AI Adoption Score is composed of three weighted dimensions:

| Dimension | Weight | Question It Answers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency** | 40% | How often do developers use AI? |
| **Depth** | 40% | How integrated is AI into actual development? |
| **Coverage** | 20% | How broadly is AI being adopted across the team? |

Click on any dimension card to view detailed analysis and improvement suggestions specific to that dimension.

Quick Reference: Score Tiers[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#quick-reference-score-tiers "Direct link to Quick Reference: Score Tiers")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Score Range | Tier | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0–20 | Minimal adoption | AI usage is sporadic or experimental |
| 21–50 | Early adoption | Some developers are using AI regularly |
| 51–75 | Growing adoption | AI is becoming part of team workflows |
| 76–90 | Strong adoption | AI is deeply integrated into development |
| 91–100 | AI-first engineering org | AI is central to how the team ships code |

Next Steps[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Understand what each dimension measures](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score)
    
*   [Learn strategies to improve your score](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/improving-your-score)
    
*   [Use the dashboard for team leadership](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/for-team-leads)
    

*   [Who Is It For?](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#who-is-it-for)

    *   [Main Score Display](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#main-score-display)
        
    *   [Timeline Visualization](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#timeline-visualization)
        
    *   [Time Period Filters](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#time-period-filters)
        
    *   [Personal vs. Organization View](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#personal-vs-organization-view)
        
    *   [Trend Indicators](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/overview#trend-indicators)

# Understanding Your AI Adoption Score | Kilo Code Docs

[Skip to main content](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The AI Adoption Score is a 0–100 metric representing how deeply and consistently your team uses AI across real development workflows. This page explains what each dimension measures and how to interpret your score.

The Three Dimensions[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#the-three-dimensions "Direct link to The Three Dimensions")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your total score is calculated from three weighted dimensions:

### Frequency (40% of total score)[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#frequency-40-of-total-score "Direct link to Frequency (40% of total score)")

**"How often do developers use AI?"**

This dimension measures the regularity of AI tool usage across your team, normalized per-user and blended across the organization.

![Frequency view of the AI Adoption Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/ai-adoption/frequency.png)

**Signals measured:**

*   Agent interactions per day
*   Autocomplete acceptance
*   Cloud Agent sessions
*   Reviewer Agent runs

**What it tells you:** Teams with high Frequency scores have made AI a daily habit—not something they reach for only on difficult problems. Low Frequency often indicates that developers haven't yet built AI into their regular workflow.

### Depth (40% of total score)[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#depth-40-of-total-score "Direct link to Depth (40% of total score)")

**"How integrated is AI into actual development?"**

This dimension captures trust and dependency—whether AI is a side tool or an integral part of how your team ships code.

![Depth view of the AI Adoption Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/ai-adoption/depth.png)

**Signals measured:**

*   Queries per hour worked
*   Percentage of AI suggestions accepted
*   AI-generated lines merged into the codebase
*   **Retention rate:** Percentage of AI-suggested lines merged unaltered
*   Multi-agent chains (coding → review → deploy)

**What it tells you:** High Depth scores indicate that developers trust AI output enough to ship it. Low Depth may mean developers are experimenting with AI but not adopting its suggestions, which could signal context or quality issues.

### Coverage (20% of total score)[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#coverage-20-of-total-score "Direct link to Coverage (20% of total score)")

**"How broadly is AI being adopted across the team?"**

This dimension captures reach and rollout—how many team members are using AI and how consistently throughout the week.

![Coverage view of the AI Adoption Dashboard](https://kilo.ai/docs/img/ai-adoption/coverage.png)

**Signals measured:**

*   Percentage of users using any AI agent weekly
*   Percentage of users adopting 2+ agents
*   Percentage adopting 4+ agents
*   Weekday usage breadth (usage throughout the week vs. concentrated on specific days)

**What it tells you:** Coverage reveals adoption gaps. A team might have power users driving high Frequency and Depth scores while other team members barely use AI at all.

Score Tiers[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#score-tiers "Direct link to Score Tiers")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your score falls into one of five tiers:

| Score Range | Tier | What It Means |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **0–20** | Minimal adoption | AI usage is sporadic or experimental. Most developers aren't using AI tools regularly. |
| **21–50** | Early adoption | Some developers have incorporated AI into their workflow, but it's not yet team-wide. |
| **51–75** | Growing adoption | AI is becoming a standard part of how the team works. Most developers use it, though depth varies. |
| **76–90** | Strong adoption | AI is deeply integrated into development workflows. Teams at this level trust and depend on AI suggestions. |
| **91–100** | AI-first engineering org | AI is central to how the team ships code. Usage is high, broad, and deeply integrated. |

How Scores Are Calculated[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#how-scores-are-calculated "Direct link to How Scores Are Calculated")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The scoring system applies several normalization techniques to produce meaningful, comparable scores:

### Per-Developer Normalization[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#per-developer-normalization "Direct link to Per-Developer Normalization")

Usage is normalized on a per-developer basis. This means a 10-person team using AI moderately will score comparably to a 50-person team using AI moderately—raw volume doesn't inflate scores.

### Outlier Capping[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#outlier-capping "Direct link to Outlier Capping")

Extreme usage by individual power users is capped to prevent a single enthusiastic developer from skewing the entire team's score.

### Rolling Window[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#rolling-window "Direct link to Rolling Window")

Scores use a **weekly rolling window** for stability. This smooths out day-to-day fluctuations while still responding to real changes in behavior.

### Multi-Source Aggregation[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#multi-source-aggregation "Direct link to Multi-Source Aggregation")

The score aggregates event streams from multiple surfaces:

*   **IDE** — Autocomplete and coding agent interactions
*   **CLI** — Terminal-based AI usage
*   **Reviewer Agent** — AI-assisted code reviews
*   **Cloud Agent** — Browser-based AI sessions

Why Scores Fluctuate[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#why-scores-fluctuate "Direct link to Why Scores Fluctuate")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your score may change from week to week for several reasons:

**Normal fluctuations:**

*   Team members on vacation or leave
*   End-of-sprint vs. start-of-sprint patterns
*   Seasonal variations (holidays, summer slowdowns)

**Meaningful changes:**

*   New team members onboarding (may temporarily lower Coverage)
*   Team members leaving the organization
*   Changes in development workflow or tooling
*   Successful adoption initiatives

Interpreting Your Score[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#interpreting-your-score "Direct link to Interpreting Your Score")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Focus on Trends, Not Absolutes[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#focus-on-trends-not-absolutes "Direct link to Focus on Trends, Not Absolutes")

The exact number matters less than the direction. A score of 45 means "early adoption, room to grow"—but whether that's good or bad depends on where you were last month and where you're headed.

### Compare Dimensions[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#compare-dimensions "Direct link to Compare Dimensions")

If your total score is low, look at which dimension is pulling it down:

*   **Low Frequency?** Focus on building daily habits
*   **Low Depth?** Work on trust and context quality
*   **Low Coverage?** Focus on onboarding and activation

### Distribution Over Average[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#distribution-over-average "Direct link to Distribution Over Average")

While the dashboard shows aggregate scores, the real insight often comes from understanding distribution. A team with a 50 score might have half the team at 80+ and half at 20—that's different from everyone at 50.

What About Individual Scores?[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#what-about-individual-scores "Direct link to What About Individual Scores?")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Individual user scores are available through the "Only my usage" toggle. However, the real value of the AI Adoption Score is in:

*   **Aggregate team metrics** — Understanding organizational trends
*   **Distribution analysis** — Identifying adoption gaps
*   **Comparative benchmarking** — Setting and tracking goals

Individual scores are most useful for personal development and self-assessment, not performance evaluation.

Next Steps[​](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Learn strategies to improve each dimension](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/improving-your-score)
    
*   [Use the dashboard for team leadership](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/for-team-leads)
    

*   [The Three Dimensions](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#the-three-dimensions)
    *   [Frequency (40% of total score)](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#frequency-40-of-total-score)
        
    *   [Depth (40% of total score)](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#depth-40-of-total-score)
        
    *   [Coverage (20% of total score)](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#coverage-20-of-total-score)
        
*   [Score Tiers](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#score-tiers)
    
*   [How Scores Are Calculated](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#how-scores-are-calculated)
    *   [Per-Developer Normalization](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#per-developer-normalization)
        
    *   [Outlier Capping](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#outlier-capping)
        
    *   [Rolling Window](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#rolling-window)
        
    *   [Multi-Source Aggregation](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#multi-source-aggregation)
        
*   [Why Scores Fluctuate](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#why-scores-fluctuate)
    
*   [Interpreting Your Score](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#interpreting-your-score)
    *   [Focus on Trends, Not Absolutes](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#focus-on-trends-not-absolutes)
        
    *   [Compare Dimensions](https://kilo.ai/docs/plans/adoption-dashboard/understanding-your-score#compare-dimensions)
